response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering fundamentals and communication skills, this role requires specific expertise that is absent from the resume. The candidate lacks recent and substantial C++ experience (last hands-on work was 10+ years ago at National Instruments), has no demonstrated Windows platform development history (all recent work is web/backend on Linux), and completely lacks capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge. The resume shows a career trajectory toward web development with JavaScript/TypeScript rather than systems programming or financial services, making this a significant skill and domain mismatch.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals with 15+ years of diverse experience across multiple languages and platforms
Proven ability to master complex technical domains and deliver in high-performance systems (e.g., real-time data systems at Dunnhumby)
Excellent communication and mentoring skills demonstrated through workshops, talks, and community contributions
concerns: Minimal C++ experience with a 10+ year gap since last hands-on work (2014-2015 at National Instruments)
No demonstrated Windows platform development experience; recent career focused on web development and Linux systems
Zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domains
